chapter_title: About the Forbiddance of Exaggeration in Punishing

hadith_entries.text:
896. Narrated Abdullah ibn Omar may Allah be pleased
with her; 'Allah's Messenger said: "A woman was punished
because she imprisoned a cat till it died. She entered Hellfire
because of it. She neither gave it food, water, nor she set it
free to eat of the vermin of the earth." (Bukhari).
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)

897. Narrated Sowayd ibn Moqarrin Al-Mozani; ‘We
were seven brothers and we had but only a female servant. One
of us slapped her and the Prophet ordered us to emancipate
her.'

898. Narrated Abu-Mas'oud Al-Ansari; 'I was hitting a
slave of mine and I heard someone calling me: "Know Abu-
Mas'oud! Know Abu-Mas'oud! I turned and saw the
Messenger of Allah .He said: "Indeed, Allah is More
Capable over you than you are over him." Abu-Mas'oud said;
'I never hit a slave since then.'
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
